provenance.source_corpus: Anuran call surveys on small wetlands in Prince Edward Island, Canada restored by dredging of sediments

source_spans.text: Wetlands were restored by means of dredging accumulated sediment and organic debris to create open water
Wetlands were restored by means of dredging accumulated sediment and organic debris to create open water
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were signi®cantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were signi®cantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands

nodes.name: Abundance indices of northern leopard frogs

source_spans.text: Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were signi®cantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were signi®cantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were signi®cantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands
The number of species calling in restored wetlands was positively correlated with proximity to freshwater rivers
in reference wetlands, the number was positively associated with forested perimeters and area of open water
in reference wetlands, the number was positively associated with forested perimeters and area of open water
Occurrence of calling green frogs in restored wetlands was positively correlated with percent cattail
Occurrence of calling green frogs in restored wetlands was positively correlated with percent cattail
in reference wetlands, with proximity to other wetlands
Wetland restoration by means of dredging accumulated organic soil and debris for the primary purpose of creating open water for waterfowl does provide habitat for anurans
Agricultural activities, such as pesticide use, have been linked to larval death and limb deformities in amphibians
Agricultural activities, such as pesticide use, have been linked to larval death and limb deformities in amphibians
Nutrient run-off and sedimentation associated with agricultural landscapes are also detrimental to anurans
Nutrient run-off and sedimentation associated with agricultural landscapes are also detrimental to anurans
